sample.name: Atera cervical SCC · section 01
sample.description: 10x Atera whole-transcriptome assay, cervical squamous cell carcinoma, one section. Cell labels are 10x's own (cell_groups.csv).
caveats: n=1 section — within-sample statistics only; not a biological claim.
Tumor cells dominate neighbor fractions — use enrichment, not raw nb_.
Browser map shows a spatial subsample of 6,000 / 547,219 labeled cells.

suggestions.rationale: Atlas cells-of-interest Wilcoxon marker score 9.30 (targetselection_outputs/top20_potential_pertubation_targets.csv). Knockout DE artifact available for LCP2 (perturb_outputs/diffexp_genes.csv).

perturbation.method: Population-level scLDM-CD4 knockout differential expression (perturb_outputs/diffexp_genes.csv) applied additively to this cell's observed marker baseline from cells.parquet. Knockout gene=LCP2. Genes absent from the DE table keep delta=0 and in_de_table=false.

top_pathways.term: Positive Regulation Of Cell Cycle Process (GO:0090068)

tool_replay.why: Rank terminally exhausted CD4_Tex_term cells in tumor_core
Surface atlas-derived knockout targets for this phenotype/niche
Replay scLDM-CD4 DE artifact for LCP2 on the focus cell baseline

atlas_mapping_meta.method: differential (Treg vs rest, within Atera) -> score_genes on atlas
atlas_mapping_meta.caveat: AIFI is HEALTHY BLOOD. Core Treg identity (FOXP3/IL2RA/CTLA4/IKZF2) transfers. The tumour-specific program does NOT — there is no blood counterpart to a tumour-margin Treg. That gap is the point, not a bug.
